data.title: Genmab and AbbVie's combination drug cuts risk of disease progression by 79% in Phase 3 trial for follicular lymphoma.
data.description: Genmab A/S announced positive outcomes from the Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 trial of subcutaneous epcoritamab in combination with rituximab and lenalidomide for adults with relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma. The study achieved its main goals of overall response rate (ORR) and progression-free survival (PFS), showing significant differences in both endpoints. The treatment reduced the risk of disease progression or death by 79%. Genmab is collaborating with AbbVie Inc. on the drug's development. The findings, based on an interim analysis, will be presented at the 67th ASH meeting and used for global regulatory submissions.

data.title: Regenx gives bi-weekly update on MCTO, plans to file 2025 Annual Filings by September 29; no defaults reported.
data.description: Regenx has issued a bi-weekly update regarding their MCTO status and anticipates submitting their 2025 Annual Filings by September 29. The company has not reported any significant alterations or defaults.

data.title: Dr. Craig Minor named Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence for nursing innovation at Houston Methodist.
data.description: In Tomball, Texas on August 8, 2025, Dr. Craig Minor was recognized in The Inner Circle as a Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence. He was commended for his significant contributions to Leading Visionary Nursing Innovation at Houston Methodist. Dr. Minor stands out for his pioneering work and leadership in the field.

data.title: Netflix's $6B investment in the UK pays off with 11% revenue growth to £1.85B in 2024, adding 50,000 jobs and producing global content.
data.description: Shares of Netflix (NASDAQ: NFLX) rose on Friday as investors were optimistic about its U.K. financial unit's performance and recent FIFA Women's World Cup deal. The company's U.K. arm saw a revenue growth of 11% in 2024, reaching 1.85 billion pounds ($2.48 billion) as it expanded in the market. According to its recent filing with Companies House, Netflix has made significant contributions to the U.K. economy by investing $6 billion, employing over 50,000 individuals, and collaborating with more than 200 producers to create popular British content globally. The streaming giant is committed to long-term investments in the U.K., with it being the second-largest investment country after the U.S..

data.title: CPS will host a conference call on August 12, 2025, at 1 p.m. ET to discuss its second quarter 2025 earnings.
data.description: Las Vegas, Nevada - On August 8, 2025, Consumer Portfolio Services, Inc. (Nasdaq: CPSS) revealed plans for a conference call scheduled on Tuesday, August 12, 2025, at 1:00 p.m. ET. The call aims to address the Company's recent operating performance in the second quarter of 2025.

data.description: Today, the Nasdaq and S&P 500 experienced notable increases in the stock market, supported by strong performances by Apple, Cisco Systems, and Alphabet. data.title: Actelis Networks secures a significant Tier 2 carrier win, expands in UK telecom market with hybrid-fiber tech.
data.description: The recent purchase showcases the significance of Actelis' hybrid-fiber technology for carrier-grade enterprise uses, boosting the Company's presence in the UK telecommunications sector. This order emphasizes Actelis's innovative technology, underlining its relevance for enterprise applications in the UK telecommunications market.

data.title: Accumulus Synergy creates Accumulus Technologies to expand regulatory platform for global therapy access.
data.description: Accumulus Synergy has spun out a new entity called Accumulus Technologies with the goal of expanding its regulatory platform and speeding up the delivery of therapies to patients worldwide. This move is aimed at enhancing access to treatments for a larger number of individuals.

data.title: Trump imposes 25% tariff on Indian imports due to Russian oil trade, doubling total tariff to 50%.
data.description: President Donald Trump has increased tariffs on Indian imports by 25%, bringing the total tariff burden on Indian goods to 50%. This decision came in response to India's purchase of Russian oil, which the White House views as a threat to U.S. national security and foreign policy interests. The announcement was made through an executive order on August 6. The move is part of efforts to address the national emergency declared over Russian aggression and its economic support network. The INDA ETF is experiencing significant movement as a result. For further details, the full story is available on the website Benzinga.com.
